item.title: Gardner Case, Coral Crisis, Power Woes
item.ns0:title: Gardner Case, Coral Crisis, Power Woes
item.description:
Tonight on Our News, explosive new court documents in a high-profile U.S. drug case reveal alleged links between seized cash and local political campaign activity as a judge denies bail for a well-known figure. We take you to the OAS General Assembly as the Foreign Affairs Minister addresses regional stability, while scientists and government officials sound a dire warning over a fast-spreading disease threatening the nation's critical coral reefs. Our team also investigates a harrowing sexual assault involving an elderly resident and brings you the latest on the mounting frustration over persistent power outages gripping Western New Providence. Join us for these headlines, the launch of a multi-million dollar blue economy initiative, and a look at the "Love Our Locals" experience at Bahamar.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns1:encoded:
Tonight on Our News, explosive new court documents in a high-profile U.S. drug case reveal alleged links between seized cash and local political campaign activity as a judge denies bail for a well-known figure. We take you to the OAS General Assembly as the Foreign Affairs Minister addresses regional stability, while scientists and government officials sound a dire warning over a fast-spreading disease threatening the nation's critical coral reefs. Our team also investigates a harrowing sexual assault involving an elderly resident and brings you the latest on the mounting frustration over persistent power outages gripping Western New Providence. Join us for these headlines, the launch of a multi-million dollar blue economy initiative, and a look at the "Love Our Locals" experience at Bahamar.
-----
Copyright Disclaimer under section 107 of the Copyright Act of 1976, allowance is made for "fair use" for purposes such as criticism, comment, news reporting, teaching, scholarship, education and research. Fair use is a use permitted by copyright statute that might otherwise be infringing.
item.ns0:summary:
Tonight on Our News, explosive new court documents in a high-profile U.S. drug case reveal alleged links between seized cash and local political campaign activity as a judge denies bail for a well-known figure. We take you to the OAS General Assembly as the Foreign Affairs Minister addresses regional stability, while scientists and government officials sound a dire warning over a fast-spreading disease threatening the nation's critical coral reefs. Our team also investigates a harrowing sexual assault involving an elderly resident and brings you the latest on the mounting frustration over persistent power outages gripping Western New Providence. Join us for these headlines, the launch of a multi-million dollar blue economy initiative, and a look at the "Love Our Locals" experience at Bahamar.
-----

item.ns0:author: Our News Bahamas
